sn45.27:1.2: “Monks, just as a pot without a stand is easy to knock over, but if it has a stand is hard to knock over;
sn45.27:1.3: in the same way, the mind without a stand is easy to knock over, but if it has a stand is hard to knock over.
sn45.27:1.5: Just this noble eightfold path, i.e.,
sn45.27:1.6: right view, right resolve, right speech, right action, right livelihood, right effort, right mindfulness, & right concentration.
sn45.27:1.8: “Just as a pot without a stand is easy to knock over, but if it has a stand is hard to knock over;
sn45.27:1.9: in the same way, the mind without a stand is easy to knock over, but if it has a stand is hard to knock over.”
